prd.oneLiner: Connect service rules, availability, bookings, notifications, changes, cancellations, waitlists, and operating outcomes.
prd.goal: Increase completed bookings and reduce operating loss from no-shows and manual schedule adjustments.
prd.whyNow: Availability, practitioner calendars, customer changes, and waitlists live across calls and calendars, making empty slots and customer commitments difficult to manage.
prd.problem: Availability, practitioner calendars, customer changes, and waitlists live across calls and calendars, making empty slots and customer commitments difficult to manage.
prd.solution: Connect service rules, availability, bookings, notifications, changes, cancellations, waitlists, and operating outcomes.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Customer booking a service and schedule operations practitioner
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Availability, practitioner calendars, customer changes, and waitlists live across calls and calendars, making empty slots and customer commitments difficult to manage.

targets.name: Appointment and service operations coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Availability, practitioner calendars, customer changes, and waitlists live across calls and calendars, making empty slots and customer commitments difficult to manage.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Availability, practitioner calendars, customer changes, and waitlists live across calls and calendars, making empty slots and customer commitments difficult to manage.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Increase completed bookings and reduce operating loss from no-shows and manual schedule adjustments.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Service, practitioner, availability, appointment, and change-request registration
Appointment, calendar, customer, practitioner, waitlist, and no-show-history lookup
Availability check, booking, confirmation, notification, rescheduling, cancellation, and waitlist handling
Booking conversion, utilization, no-show, waitlist, cancellation, and practitioner reporting
Service, practitioner, availability, appointment, and change-request registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
